title: Proposing an amendment to the Constitution of the United States to require that the Supreme Court of the United States be composed of nine justices.
plain_summary: This bill wants to change the Constitution to make sure the Supreme Court always has exactly nine judges. Right now, the Constitution doesn't say how many judges the Supreme Court must have. To make this change happen, two-thirds of both parts of Congress and three-fourths of all the states would have to agree. The states would have seven years to decide if they want this change.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Joint Resolution
sections.text: This joint resolution proposes to change the United States Constitution. It requires both the Senate and House of Representatives to approve it by a two-thirds vote in each chamber. The proposed change would become part of the Constitution only if the legislatures of three-fourths of all states approve it within seven years after Congress submits it to them. The amendment would add one simple rule to the Constitution: The Supreme Court of the United States must have exactly nine justices.
